food_name: Maggi Noodle with Prawn (Mi Goi Xao Tom)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Maggi instant noodle stir-fried with prawn. FOODON_03311069 (noodle dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Noodles, cooked) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for instant noodle stir-fry with prawn in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific prepared noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Maggi Noodle with Prawn is approximately 350 g, comprising a full plate of stir-fried instant noodles with several medium-sized prawns, egg, and aromatics.

nutrient_source.title: Instant noodles, cooked, with seafood — adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Noodles, chinese, cellophane or long rice (mung beans), cooked) and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia; prawn contribution estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 15150 (Crustaceans, shrimp, mixed species, cooked, moist heat); seasoning sodium estimated from published Maggi noodle seasoning packet data cross-referenced with USDA Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central / FAO INFOODS SEA
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Maggi instant noodle stir-fried with prawn exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA SR Legacy cooked wheat noodles (NDB 20113) for the noodle base, (2) USDA FoodData Central cooked shrimp (NDB 15150) for prawn contribution, (3) published Maggi seasoning packet sodium content, and (4) FAO/INFOODS SEA stir-fried noodle dish analogues. Proportions estimated as approximately 60% noodle, 20% prawn, 10% egg, 10% oil and seasoning by weight. Values represent a composite per-100g estimate.

gi_source.title: Instant noodles GI — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Instant noodles (wheat-based, boiled) GI ~52 (glucose reference).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 52 is based on published GI values for wheat-based instant noodles (boiled/stir-fried) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables. Stir-frying with oil and protein (prawn, egg) further attenuates glycemic response; GI 52 is therefore a conservative upper-bound estimate for this dish. GL per serving calculated as (52 × 19.5 g carbohydrate per 100g × 350g serving / 100) / 100 = approximately 35.5 / 100 × 52 = 19.9.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100g; ~1820 mg per serving) primarily from Maggi seasoning packet and soy/oyster sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load per serving (~1820 mg, approaching the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg); requesting reduced seasoning or sauce is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) with moderate carbohydrate content; portion size significantly affects glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume this dish in controlled portions (150–200 g) as the GI of ~52 is in the low range, but the full restaurant serving (~350 g) delivers approximately 68 g of carbohydrate, which may cause postprandial glucose elevation and should be accounted for in meal planning.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from seasoning and cooking oil; prawn contributes dietary cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the high sodium content (~1820 mg per serving) and moderate cholesterol contribution from prawn (~252 mg per serving) warrant portion control and preference for reduced-sodium preparation; the dish's prawn content provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids that partially offset cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Maggi Noodle with Prawn, known in Vietnamese as Mì Gói Xào Tôm (Mi Goi Xao Tom), is a popular stir-fried instant noodle dish served at Vietnamese-influenced restaurants in Singapore, such as Thien Long Vietnam Restaurant. The dish is prepared by stir-frying pre-cooked Maggi brand wheat-based instant noodles in a hot wok with fresh or frozen prawns, egg, garlic, spring onion, soy sauce, and oyster sauce, with the Maggi seasoning packet incorporated for flavour. The result is a savoury, slightly smoky noodle dish with a tender-chewy texture, umami-rich sauce coating, and plump prawns. It is a fusion of Vietnamese home-cooking tradition (using instant noodles as a quick meal base) and Singaporean hawker-style wok cooking. Nutritionally, a standard restaurant plate (~350 g) provides approximately 518 kcal, 27 g protein, 16 g fat, and 68 g carbohydrate. The dish is notably high in sodium (~1820 mg per serving) due to the Maggi seasoning packet and sauces, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index of the wheat-based instant noodle base is approximately 52 (low GI), moderated further by the protein and fat content from prawn and egg. Prawns contribute lean protein, iodine, and some omega-3 fatty acids. The dish contains gluten (wheat noodles), crustaceans (prawn), egg, and soy allergens. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on a low-sodium diet without modification.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific dish (Maggi instant noodle stir-fried with prawn) was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA primary databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (cooked wheat noodles, cooked shrimp, egg) and FAO/INFOODS SEA stir-fried noodle analogues. GI is estimated from published University of Sydney GI Database values for wheat-based instant noodles. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cooked wheat noodles NDB 20113; cooked shrimp NDB 15150), FAO/INFOODS SEA stir-fried noodle dish analogues, and published Maggi seasoning packet sodium data. No single approved-source entry exactly matches this dish. 2. Sodium estimate (~520 mg/100g) is considered conservative given the Maggi seasoning packet typically contributes 800–1000 mg sodium per packet; actual restaurant preparation may be higher. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in wheat noodle and prawn components per USDA data. 5. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on wheat-based instant noodles from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008; stir-frying with protein and fat is expected to attenuate glycemic response. GL per serving = (52 × 68.25 g carb per 350g serving) / 100 = 35.5, rounded to 19.9 per 100g equivalent for per-serving GL field (GL per serving = 52 × 19.5/100 × 350/100 = 19.9 — note: field stores GL for the stated serving size of 350g). 6. Halal status is marked false as the restaurant (Thien Long Vietnam Restaurant) is not confirmed halal-certified; verification required. 7. This dish is served at a Vietnamese restaurant in Singapore and represents a Vietnamese-Singaporean fusion preparation. Multilingual names reflect both Singaporean mandatory languages (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) and Vietnamese as the dish's culinary origin language.
